user_message: Give me the QBR preparation for my team. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 12:26 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Alpine Ski House

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 12:55 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 12:31 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-01-05
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:05:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:07:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
